food.name: Bacon

identity.basisReason: Raw per-100g as-sold basis matches package-label style nutrition and avoids cooked-water-loss distortion.
includes: plain cured sliced bacon as sold

scoreReadiness.summary: Core raw bacon nutrition is coherent enough for launch scripting, but a few protein-quality style fields are still older calibration proxies rather than locked source facts.
blockers: collagen and protein-quality proxy metrics still need either exact-source backing or deliberate removal
cured bacon product variability means exact sodium-forward real-world burden is broader than this simplified nutrition record captures

sourceNotes: Identity locked to cured pork bacon, unprepared, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with USDA SR Legacy style pork bacon cured unprepared entries.
Raw basis kept intentionally, not cooked bacon values.

sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 37.1g of fat. Saturated fat is a major pressure point. For meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
sections.subtitleText: 37.1g of fat. Saturated fat is a major pressure point. For meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 0g of carbs. Everything else is lackluster. In this category, carbs barely matter.
sections.subtitleText: 0g of carbs. Everything else is lackluster. In this category, carbs barely matter.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 13.7g of protein. Protein quantity is doing most of the work. Strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
sections.subtitleText: 13.7g of protein. Protein quantity is doing most of the work. Strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: Vitamin b12 at 21% daily value stands out most. That is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: Vitamin b12 at 21% daily value stands out most. That is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: Zinc at 10% daily value stands out most. Useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: Zinc at 10% daily value stands out most. Useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: Pros first: highly palatable and appealing. A real-world reason people love it. Works as a strong flavour booster. Useful in meals even in smaller amounts. Has strong comfort-food appeal. Popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

sections.subtitleText: Pros first: highly palatable and appealing. A real-world reason people love it. Works as a strong flavour booster. Useful in meals even in smaller amounts. Has strong comfort-food appeal. Popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: The drawbacks next: sodium is a major drawback. A central practical health issue for bacon. Processing is a major negative. A key downside now that more processed foods are in scope. Rarely improves a meal much on its own. Usually works more as a flavour add-on than a genuinely strong meat choice.

closing.summary: Bacon does have a few real-world upsides. But the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. Best only if convenience, budget, or taste matters more than getting the cleanest meat option.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only if convenience, budget, or taste matters more than getting the cleanest meat option.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

narrationBlocks.text: 37.1g of fat. Saturated fat is a major pressure point. For meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. Everything else is lackluster. In this category, carbs barely matter.
13.7g of protein. Protein quantity is doing most of the work. Strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
Vitamin b12 at 21% daily value stands out most. That is barely moving the needle.
Zinc at 10% daily value stands out most. Useful, but still pretty limited.

Pros first: highly palatable and appealing. A real-world reason people love it. Works as a strong flavour booster. Useful in meals even in smaller amounts. Has strong comfort-food appeal. Popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

The drawbacks next: sodium is a major drawback. A central practical health issue for bacon. Processing is a major negative. A key downside now that more processed foods are in scope. Rarely improves a meal much on its own. Usually works more as a flavour add-on than a genuinely strong meat choice.

Bacon does have a few real-world upsides. But the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. Best only if convenience, budget, or taste matters more than getting the cleanest meat option.

explanation.summary: Pros are carrying this food most, while cons are holding it back most. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 22.4. Section scores: fats 48.9, proteins 20, vitamins 12, minerals 3.8, pros 50, cons 0.
